notes: Cl-only Li5.4 composition. No Br. Control for isolating Br effect.

postprocessing.V0_coords: v103 Basin A relaxed coords in v102.6 cell

elastic_dft_0K_GPa.notes: C11(85.3)≈comp3(85.6)≈comp4(85.5). C44(28.0)≈comp3(27.5). E(73.3)>comp3(71.7)→Cl-only slightly more rigid.

elastic_mlip_600K_GPa.notes: Sign-corrected. Highest E of all comps! Cl-only = most rigid.

basin_B.source: v106 coords relaxed in v103 cell

eos_attempt.pattern: Basin B stable only at odd volumes, collapses at even volumes (50+ atoms rearrange)

basin_B.conclusion: Basin B is 1.4 meV more stable but EOS impossible. Paper uses Basin A (B0=21.7 GPa). Basin B NOT mentioned in paper.
basin_B.paper_strategy: DO NOT mention Basin B. Reasons: (1) 16.6 meV = 0.013% energy = within DFT error (PBE, k-point, PP), (2) Basin A is valid local minimum (both may coexist experimentally), (3) mentioning Basin B invites reviewer question 'why not use it?' with weak answer 'EOS impossible', (4) comp5 basin discussion already covers Li ordering sensitivity. Keep Model C simple.

v108_basin.notes: 28/62 atoms moved. Li18 largest (Dx=-0.145, Dy=+0.219). Cl also moves unlike comp5.

comparison_to_comp3.interpretation: No Br -> more rigid. Confirms Br weakening effect.
